broader.creator: Respa Mindfulness
broader.caption: The Buteyko breathing technique is a powerful way to support your respiratory health and overall well-being.  Here’s how advanced Buteyko breathwork can make a difference: ✨ Improves Oxygen Efficiency– By training your body to use oxygen more efficiently, Buteyko breathwork can help boost endurance and reduce breathlessness during physical activity. ✨ Reduces Stress and Anxiety– Slow, controlled breathing signals your body to relax, lowering stress levels and promoting a sense of calm. ✨ Supports Respiratory Health– Regular practice can help reduce symptoms of asthma, improve lung function, and support long-term respiratory health. ✨ Enhances Focus and Mental Clarity– Controlled breathing helps balance CO2 levels, leading to improved concentration and cognitive function. ✨ Increases Energy Levels– By optimizing oxygen intake, Buteyko breathwork can help you feel more energized and reduce fatigue. If you’re ready to dive deeper into the power of your breath, give Buteyko a try and experience these benefits for yourself! 🌱✨ . . #buteykobreathwork #breathebetter #oxygenefficiency #mentalclarity #StressRelief #breathwork #respa #breathe #anxiety #MentalHealth #energylevels

broader.creator: Dylan Petkus MD (Inactive)
broader.caption: Yoga can make sleep apnea worse — unless you tweak this one thing. (Tap @apneareset to get your clear, personalized path to better sleep) Here’s where it goes off the rails. Many yoga classes use fast breathing patterns. Think rapid inhales and forceful exhales. But at night, sleep apnea already creates that pattern. You stop breathing, then gasp and overbreathe. That fast breathing lowers carbon dioxide too much. Low CO₂ makes the airway more unstable. Unstable airway means more collapse. More collapse means more exhaustion. So what should you do instead? 1️⃣ Slow nasal breathing. Gentle, controlled breaths increase airway stability. 2️⃣ Light, quiet exhales. This keeps carbon dioxide in a healthier range. 3️⃣ Neck and upper airway mobility work. Stretching and posture drills support airway space. The calming effect of yoga is powerful. Downshifting your nervous system improves sleep depth. Just skip the hyperventilation. Slow breathing builds a more stable airway tonight. That’s a helpful tweak — but it’s not the full fix. To actually correct sleep apnea, your breathing strategy must match your physiology and collapse pattern. Tap @apneareset to get your clear, personalized path to better sleep

broader.caption: i was so wrong about this and i need to apologize!! A while back, Intake Breathing sent me mouth tape. I tried it one night, didn’t like it, and forgot about it.
